hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The instruction gives only observable audit symptoms; the solver must inspect and run the Rust plan parser, queue runner, branch filter, and emitters to learn the real state model.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: A correct repair spans admission, bounded ordering, removed-branch filtering, parent/depth transfer, and side-channel output. No one module can satisfy the verifier.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose
hardness_axes.reasoning: The prompt describes stalls and inconsistent generated artifacts, not which internal operation drops or leaks work.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Changing queue order changes accepted turns and artifact order; changing branch filtering changes child depth and duplicate suppression, so local edits affect distant checks.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: The project is a bespoke deterministic executor simulator, not a stock async runtime or library recipe, so the solver must reason from local code and traces.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Even an honest behavior contract leaves plan semantics, state transitions, and repair locations to discover in the codebase.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: All six cases combine overlapping symptoms; the repair is not finding one bad file or row.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The verifier rebuilds and reruns the binary and compares generated JSON, JSONL, and text artifacts, so patching one output cannot pass.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Expected records are re-derived from multiple case plans with different capacity, branch, duplicate, and child patterns.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The prompt can be complete about observable outputs without naming spill-forward scheduling, descendant filtering, or fix modules.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Hardness comes from code diagnosis and coupled state repair, not from anti-cheating wrappers.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Verifier dependency placement and metadata are routine; the semantic Rust repair is the challenge.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A one-file patch cannot simultaneously fix bounded pressure, cancelled descendants, child depth, duplicates, and output agreement.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The oracle is planned across at least admission, ordering, gate, host, flow, and drive roots rather than a small wholesale replacement.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: There is no small policy table or manifest to edit; correctness comes from running state transitions.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix-path roots and symbols are opaque and avoid instruction nouns such as audit, queue, branch, child, record, lane, and journal.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Fix-path functions use opaque names and decoy helpers rhyme with real modules, so helper names do not reveal prompt bullets.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task is not implementing a standard channel, waker, or cancellation recipe; it is repairing this simulator's invariants.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No security or daemon aura is used as difficulty; the challenge is systems debugging.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The requirements are coupled because accepted turns, branch filtering, depth fields, and side-channel order influence each other.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The harness is a single local container with no runtime internet; Docker realism is not counted as hardness.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A frontier model still needs to run cases and correlate plans with generated artifacts before it can identify a correct patch.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The design targets hard-only acceptance; if empirical model results later exceed the hard threshold under the announced order, it must be redesigned.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The solver must discover plan ancestry, capacity spill behavior, descendant exclusion, and shared emitter ordering from code/runtime behavior.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The instruction remains symptoms-only and does not reveal algorithms, file locations, thresholds, or causes.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three plausible fix topologies each require at least three coordinated code locations, proving the idea is not a one-function locus.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: Pytest deterministically rebuilds and runs local Rust code, then checks generated artifacts against Python-derived expected traces.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The public contract identifies command, output path, fields, and clean-audit properties without implementation hints.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An expert can solve it in hours by running cases and tracing state; no external service or huge rewrite is required.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: It requires professional Rust debugging across scheduling, cancellation, hierarchy, and artifact consistency.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Executor liveness under cancellation and bounded queues is a realistic systems reliability problem.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: The verifier grades produced audit behavior and artifacts, not a prescribed patch process.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md gives the observable audit contract, but not the plan grammar, bounded drain semantics, descendant filtering, depth propagation, or generated side-channel paths.

discovery_budget.discovery: Plan rows create a parent graph whose accepted child depth must be derived from the surviving ancestor chain.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming the ancestry propagation rule as the cause would point directly at the repair path and collapse diagnosis.

discovery_budget.discovery: Bounded lane pressure is a spill-forward process, not a drop-on-overflow process, and accepted turns come from the drain cycle.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Stating spill-forward scheduling would give the core algorithm and turn repair away.

discovery_budget.discovery: Cancelled branch names must exclude descendants as well as the named row, and this interacts with duplicate task admission.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: The instruction should say cancelled branches stay out of the trace, not specify the internal descendant walk.

discovery_budget.discovery: The JSON trace, JSONL ledger, and text journal are generated through different emit paths that must receive the same ordered records.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: If the prompt names the divergent emitter as the issue, the solver can patch outputs without understanding runner state.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: The instruction describes a stalled or inconsistent audit and observable output properties while avoiding internal module names, algorithms, thresholds, and causes.

attack_path.summary: Run the matrix, compare failed cases with plan files, trace rows through the simulator, and coordinate repairs across admission, ordering, filtering, recovery, and emission.
attack_path.reasoning: This requires discovering behavior from code and generated artifacts, not transcribing the prompt.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Correct branch exclusion, duplicate-aware admission, spill-forward lane draining, depth propagation, and side-channel output agreement across several Rust files.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: Any smaller patch leaves cancelled descendants, lost overflow work, wrong turns, bad parent/depth fields, or artifact mismatches.

collapse_audit.reasoning: The repair frontier is distributed across opaque roots and the instruction does not enumerate internal algorithms or per-case answers.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: The solver must reconstruct the runner's state model, connect bounded pressure with turn assignment, preserve branch semantics under child work, and keep all emitted surfaces ordered identically.

renames_during_drafting.reason: The original name contained the instruction noun record.
The original name contained the instruction noun lane.
The original name contained the instruction noun branches.
The original name contained the instruction noun journal.
